LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JUAN JORGE'S MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS ENTITLED TO ENTRY OF JUDGMENT FOR THE SETTLEMENT AMOUNT, BUT NOT FOR ANY FEES OR COSTS SINCE THE AGREEMENT DOES NOT PROVIDE FOR AN AWARD OF FEES OR COSTS AND SECTION 128.5 (MISTAKENLY REFERRED TO IN PLAINTIFF'S MEMORANDUM AS 128.6) IS INAPPLICABLE TO THIS CASE. THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT SUBMITTED WITH THE MOVING PAPERS AND CONVERTED ONE COPY OF THE PROPOSED JUDGMENT INTO AN ORDER AND SIGNED IT, BUT NEITHER THE JUDGMENT NOR THE ORDER INCLUDED ANY FEES OR COSTS. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

Mtn For Entry Of Judgmnt
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF JUAN JORGE'S Motion For Entry Of Judgment. Granted in part. Plaintiff is entitled to entry of judgment for the settlement amount, but not for any fees or costs since the agreement does not provide for an award of fees or costs and section 128.5 (mistakenly referred to in plaintiff's memorandum as 128.6) is inapplicable to this case.
The court signed the proposed judgment submitted with the moving papers and converted one copy of the proposed judgment into an order and signed it, but neither the judgment nor the order included any fees or costs. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
